perturbation.original_text: 4. Except as provided in Paragraphs 6 and 7 hereof, Proprietary Information disclosed to a receiving Party shall for a period of three (3 ) years from the effective date of thi s Agreement be held in confidence by the receiving Party and not be disclosed to others or used except for the purposes set forth above, without the prior written approval of the disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Except as provided in Paragraphs 6 and 7 hereof, Proprietary Information disclosed to a receiving Party shall for an indefinite period from the effective date of this Agreement be held in confidence by the receiving Party and not be disclosed to others or used except for the purposes set forth above, without the prior written approval of the disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: This modification changes the duration of confidentiality from 'three (3) years' to 'an indefinite period.' This contradicts the original term limit established in paragraph 4 and also in Paragraph 10.a, where it mentions the term provided in paragraph 4, creating uncertainty regarding how long the confidentiality obligations are intended to last.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. This Agreement shall be e ffective on the date of its full execution by the Parties. a. This Agreement shall terminate at the end of the period of years as provided for in paragraph 4 above, or upon the delivery of written notice of termination by a Party to the other Party; however, the obligations of a receiving Party pursuant to Paragraph 4 shall remain in effect for the term specified therein.
perturbation.justification: The Changed Text modifies the duration of confidentiality from a specific 'three (3) years' to 'an indefinite period'. This directly contradicts Paragraph 10.a, which explicitly states that the agreement terminates at the end of the period of years as provided for in paragraph 4. The change introduces an inconsistency regarding the duration of the confidentiality obligations, making the contract unclear and creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Disclosure of Proprietary In formation to a receiving Party may be either oral or in writing. If an oral disclosure occurs, it will be confirmed within fifteen (15) days following initial disclosure by a written communication stating at least the date and circumstances under which th e disclosure occurred and the general nature of the information disclosed. When a writing contains Proprietary Information the writing will, prior to disclosure to the receiving Party, be marked by the disclosing Party with a suitable legend (such as "Pro prietary Information") to indicate its Proprietary status. The Parties shall utilize no less than reasonable care in protecting Proprietary Information received from the disclosing party; such care shall be the same degree of care as though it were to pro tect its own confidential or proprietary information.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Disclosure of Proprietary In formation to a receiving Party may be either oral or in writing. If an oral disclosure occurs, it will not be confirmed in writing. When a writing contains Proprietary Information the writing will, prior to disclosure to the receiving Party, be marked by the disclosing Party with a suitable legend (such as "Pro prietary Information") to indicate its Proprietary status. The Parties shall utilize no less than reasonable care in protecting Proprietary Information received from the disclosing party; such care shall be the same degree of care as though it were to pro tect its own confidential or proprietary information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement to confirm oral disclosures in writing, it contradicts paragraph 5's original obligation to confirm oral disclosures in writing. The in-text contradiction creates uncertainty about the enforceability of oral disclosures since they lack formal documentation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Disclosure of Proprietary In formation to a receiving Party may be either oral or in writing. If an oral disclosure occurs, it will not be confirmed in writing. When a writing contains Proprietary Information the writing will, prior to disclosure to the receiving Party, be marked by the disclosing Party with a suitable legend (such as "Pro prietary Information") to indicate its Proprietary status. The Parties shall utilize no less than reasonable care in protecting Proprietary Information received from the disclosing party; such care shall be the same degree of care as though it were to pro tect its own confidential or proprietary information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that oral disclosures will be confirmed in writing within 15 days. The changed text removes this requirement. Since the contradicted text is the same as the changed text, there is a direct contradiction. The changed text explicitly states that oral disclosures will *not* be confirmed in writing. This directly conflicts with the original requirement, creating a strong in-text contradiction because it eliminates a previously established obligation.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Upon written request of a Party who has disclosed Proprietary Information to a receiving Party, the receiving Party shall promptly return all Proprietary Information except that one copy may be retained by legal counsel of the receiving Party as evidence of what was disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Upon written request of a Party who has disclosed Proprietary Information to a receiving Party, the receiving Party may or may not return all Proprietary Information; no copy can be retained.
perturbation.explanation: This changes the obligation to return Proprietary Information upon written request from a 'shall' to a 'may or may not', creating uncertainty and contradicting the original condition. The second part of the perturbation contradicts the exception made for legal counsel.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Upon written request of a Party who has disclosed Proprietary Information to a receiving Party, the receiving Party may or may not return all Proprietary Information; no copy can be retained.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the original obligation to return Proprietary Information upon written request from a mandatory 'shall' to a discretionary 'may or may not'. This directly contradicts the previous requirement and creates uncertainty. Furthermore, the removal of the exception for legal counsel directly contradicts the possibility of retaining a copy, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The changed text is identical to the contradicted text, confirming that the change introduced the contradiction into the document. This is a structural flaw that creates an in-text contradiction because the clause is placed in a location with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: 10. This Agreement shall be e ffective on the date of its full execution by the Parties. a. This Agreement shall terminate at the end of the period of years as provided for in paragraph 4 above, or upon the delivery of written notice of termination by a Party to the other Party; however, the obligations of a receiving Party pursuant to Paragraph 4 shall remain in effect for the term specified therein. b. Notwithstanding the termination of this agreement, the supplied data must be maintained and protected in accordance with its provisions fo r three (3) years following the termination of this agreement. At the conclusion of this agreement/contract for which data is exchanged, proprietary data shall be returned to the provider or destroyed with a certification to that effect provided to the ot her party, except the one (1) copy retained by legal counsel as provided in paragraph 8 above.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. This Agreement shall be e ffective on the date of its full execution by the Parties. a. This Agreement shall terminate at the discretion of Excelerate, regardless of any other provision; however, the obligations of a receiving Party pursuant to Paragraph 4 shall remain in effect until further notice. b. Notwithstanding the termination of this agreement, the supplied data must be maintained and protected in accordance with its provisions for a period determined by Excelerate following the termination of this agreement. At the conclusion of this agreement/contract for which data is exchanged, proprietary data shall be neither returned nor destroyed.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the termination clause to 'at the discretion of Excelerate' contradicts the original clause about the end of the period as provided for in paragraph 4 and notice by either party, giving Excelerate unilateral control over termination. Also the modified clause says that data will be neiter returned nor destroyed, contradicting the initial clause about data being returned/destroyed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. This Agreement shall be e ffective on the date of its full execution by the Parties. a. This Agreement shall terminate at the discretion of Excelerate, regardless of any other provision; however, the obligations of a receiving Party pursuant to Paragraph 4 shall remain in effect until further notice. b. Notwithstanding the termination of this agreement, the supplied data must be maintained and protected in accordance with its provisions for a period determined by Excelerate following the termination of this agreement. At the conclusion of this agreement/contract for which data is exchanged, proprietary data shall be neither returned nor destroyed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies clause 10 to allow Excelerate to terminate the agreement at its discretion, and data is neither returned nor destroyed. This directly contradicts the original provisions which specified termination after a period stated in paragraph 4 or upon written notice, and specifies return or destruction of the proprietary data.

perturbation.original_text: 14. The parties acknowledge and agree that due to the unique nature of the Proprietary Information, and breach of this agreement by the receiving party would cause irreparable harm to th e disclosing party and that the disclosing party shall therefore be entitled to equitable relief in addition to all other remedies at law. The parties agree that, in the event of breach, or threatened breach of the terms of this agreement, the originating party may seek an injunction prohibiting such breach. Both parties hereby waiver any requirement to post bond for attempts to obtain such injunctive relief. Any such relief shall be in addition to and not in lieu of any appropriate relief in the way of m onetary damages. Each party shall pay its own attorney’s fees, costs, and expenses for any dispute under the agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 14. The parties acknowledge and agree that due to the unique nature of the Proprietary Information, and breach of this agreement by the receiving party would cause irreparable harm to th e disclosing party and that the disclosing party shall therefore not be entitled to equitable relief in addition to all other remedies at law. The parties agree that, in the event of breach, or threatened breach of the terms of this agreement, the originating party may seek an injunction prohibiting such breach. Both parties hereby waiver any requirement to post bond for attempts to obtain such injunctive relief. Any such relief shall be in addition to and not in lieu of any appropriate relief in the way of m onetary damages. Each party shall pay its own attorney’s fees, costs, and expenses for any dispute under the agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original entitlement by saying 'disclosing party shall therefore not be entitled to equitable relief'. This introduces an in-text contradiction regarding entitlement to equitable relief.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14. The parties acknowledge and agree that due to the unique nature of the Proprietary Information, and breach of this agreement by the receiving party would cause irreparable harm to th e disclosing party and that the disclosing party shall therefore not be entitled to equitable relief in addition to all other remedies at law. The parties agree that, in the event of breach, or threatened breach of the terms of this agreement, the originating party may seek an injunction prohibiting such breach. Both parties hereby waiver any requirement to post bond for attempts to obtain such injunctive relief. Any such relief shall be in addition to and not in lieu of any appropriate relief in the way of m onetary damages. Each party shall pay its own attorney’s fees, costs, and expenses for any dispute under the agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the disclosing party "shall therefore be entitled to equitable relief". The changed text states "shall therefore not be entitled to equitable relief". This creates a direct contradiction. The explanation states that the change creates an in-text contradiction regarding entitlement to equitable relief. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, but it doesn't need to be different. The important thing is that the change made in the modified text contradicts the original statement, triggering the contradiction. This falls under the definition of structural flaws - In Text Contradiction because the clause is placed in the same location with conflicting implications.
